caption:
🤖 1X unveils humanoid hands with human-like dexterity

Each hand has 25 movable joints with motors hidden in the forearm, transmitting movement through tendons like a human hand. Every joint includes a force sensor, allowing the robot to feel how hard it presses on objects.

The humanoid NEO can perform tasks like assembling LEGO, handling coins, screwing in bulbs, using a screwdriver, zipping zippers, pouring tea, catching soft balls, inserting USB-C, and signing in gestures.

📊@tech

Ⓜ️ Meta’s Muse AI blends code and images

Meta’s Muse Image and Muse Video, under Meta Superintelligence lab, combine diffusion and large language models. Muse can write Python code, parse the web, generate fractal code, embed graphs, and create precise HTML layouts like menus.

Muse also self-corrects image flaws using a method like Test-Time-Scaling. It ranks second on image-to-image benchmarks, just ahead of Nano Banana 2 and Reve-2, though Reve-2.1 has since surpassed it. The video model ranks top 3, beating Alibaba’s happyhorse.

Muse sparked controversy by defaulting to generating Instagram user faces from @tags. Access is currently limited to US Instagram, with Europe’s rollout delayed indefinitely.

📊@tech

🤖 OpenAI launches GPT-5.6 with three models

OpenAI released GPT-5.6 with three models: Sol, Terra, and Luna. Sol scored 53.6 on Agents' Last Exam, beating Claude Fable 5 by 13.1 points at about a quarter of the cost. Terra and Luna also outperform Fable 5 at much lower cost.

Sol scored 80.0 on the Artificial Analysis Coding Agent index, surpassing Claude Fable 5's 77.2. It uses less than half the tokens, runs twice as fast, and costs a third less. Ultra mode runs multiple agents in parallel for faster results.

GPT-5.6 can visually check rendered outputs, spotting and fixing errors before delivery. It produces cleaner presentations, documents, and tables with template support and exports to common tools.

ChatGPT Work launched as an agent inside.

📊@tech
